content:
Tungsten Carbide (WC) is a critical material in laser cleaning applications due to its exceptional hardness and thermal stability. Often used in nozzles and protective components, WC withstands intense laser-induced heat and abrasive conditions. Its high melting point and wear resistance make it ideal for industrial settings like surface preparation of metals or coatings removal. However, care must be taken as micro-cracking can occur under extreme thermal shock—something I’ve seen in high-power laser setups. Below are key material properties for Tungsten Carbide relevant to laser cleaning applications.

| Property                  | Value                     |
|---------------------------|---------------------------|
| Hardness (Vickers)        | 1600–2200 HV             |
| Melting Point             | ~2870°C                  |
| Thermal Conductivity      | 84.2 W/m·K               |
| Density                   | 15.63 g/cm³              |
| Compressive Strength      | 5300–7000 MPa            |
| Fracture Toughness        |
